title: Green Space Configuration and Pluvial Flood Risk
text:
Introduction
This report investigates how the spatial configuration of green spaces relates to pluvial flood risk in Delft and Xi’an. The study compares both cities using a common grid-based approach, where green-space metrics, impervious surface indicators, and flood-related indicators are calculated per grid cell.
The main research question is:
How does the spatial configuration of green spaces influence pluvial flooding in urban areas such as Delft and Xi’an?
